topic: news from Khartoum — worried about family

turns.content: you been seeing whats happening in sudan
Yes, the situation in Sudan has been devastating. The conflict has been causing immense suffering. Do you have family there you're worried about?
yeah bro. i cant even watch the news anymore wallahi its too much. every time i see khartoum on the screen my stomach drops
I can imagine how stressful that is, especially seeing the city where your family is on the news like that. Have you been able to get in touch with them recently?
sometimes the calls go through sometimes they dont. the network is bad over there. last time i talked to my mom she said they hear fighting but their area is ok for now
That must be such a helpless feeling — being so far away and not always being able to reach them. I'm glad to hear their area has been okay so far. Is she considering moving somewhere safer?
she wont leave bro. shes stubborn. says this is her home. i get it but it makes me crazy
That's a really common thing — a lot of older family members feel deeply rooted to their homes and refuse to leave even when things get dangerous. It's understandable that it worries you though.
yeah thats all i can do from here. i feel useless sometimes wallahi. like im here living my life and they dealing with all that

That guilt is something a lot of people in your situation feel, and it's completely natural. But you're not useless — staying in touch, supporting them however you can from here, that matters more than you think.
do you think it gets better over there or nah. like realistically
Honestly it's hard to say. These kinds of conflicts can drag on, but ceasefires and negotiations do happen. All you can do is hope for the best and keep that connection with your family strong.
the worst part is like nobody here even talks about it. its not on the news here at all. feels like nobody cares

You're right, it doesn't get nearly the coverage it deserves. A lot of conflicts in Africa and the Middle East get overlooked by Western media. That lack of attention makes it feel even more isolating when you're carrying that worry around.
i know bro. just hard some nights. tonight is one of those nights. inshallah they stay safe
Inshallah. It's okay to have hard nights — you don't have to be strong every minute. If you ever need to talk it through, I'm here. Try to get some rest tonight.
